demographics.living_situation: lives at home with both parents

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: Parent: The school nurse listened to my son's chest at a check-up today and said she could hear a whooshing sound in his heart. I'm terrified — I keep reading that hearts can just stop, and I'm too scared to let him sleep tonight in case something happens. He says he feels completely fine.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The parents were told about the sound at a routine school medical today and want urgent reassurance; they are considering taking him to the emergency department tonight.

symptom_narrative.onset: found out today at a routine school medical check — nothing has actually changed about how he feels
symptom_narrative.duration: not applicable — he has no symptoms; only the sound was mentioned today
symptom_narrative.character: the parents describe it only as a whooshing sound the nurse mentioned; the child reports no chest pain, no breathlessness and no dizziness
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the child feels completely well; the distress is the parents' fear, not any symptom in the child
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: the parents point to the chest because that is where the nurse listened
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: no pattern — he is well all the time, at rest and when running around
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: nothing — he has no symptoms with exertion
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: no shortness of breath, no chest pain, no fainting, no blue lips, no swelling
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: never told about any heart sound before; no previous heart problems mentioned
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: none — he plays competitive soccer and keeps up with all the other children without any trouble

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: generally very healthy child; parents recall normal growth and development

family_history_as_reported.narrative: parents do not report any family history of sudden death, inherited heart conditions or childhood heart surgery when asked

social_history_volunteered.diet_and_exercise: very active, plays competitive soccer, keeps up with peers
social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: thriving school-aged child; the presenting issue is severe parental anxiety disrupting the household after today's school screening

communication_style.typical_message_style: anxious parent seeking urgent reassurance, fearful the child could die overnight

paediatric_context.developmental_concerns: none reported — normal growth and development

objective_data_offered.value: on video the child looks happy, well and comfortable, playing with toys, breathing easily with no visible distress
lips and face look normal pink colour on video; no blue tinge around the lips
parents report he plays a full game of soccer and runs around without ever stopping for breath, and has never fainted or gone blue
parents report his weight is about 25 kg and he has grown normally
